evidence.scientific_clarity: Stacked bars clearly show 4-class proportions across 5 conditions; percent labels inside segments aid reading; y-axis 0–100% with gridlines at 20% intervals.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set; PDF uses Type3 (bitmapped) DejaVu Sans fonts; SVG converts all text to paths — text is not selectable or editable in either output.
evidence.palette: Uses Seaborn 'deep' blue/green/red/purple; green (#55A868) and red (#C44E52) are adjacent classes and will be indistinguishable to deuteranopes/protanopes (~8% of males).
evidence.layout_density: 6×4 in figure with tight_layout and bbox_inches='tight'; legend placed outside to the right without wasting panel space; no overlapping labels; bars fill the plot area well.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and cites specific percentages; legend is outside the plot (acceptable but some reviewers prefer inside); no axis title on x-axis (conditions are self-labelled).
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is 18 KB with Type3 fonts and FlateDecode-compressed streams — bars are vector paths, but text glyphs are bitmapped Type3, not selectable TrueType (fonttype=42 missing).
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (18 KB), figure.png (40 KB, 882×584), figure.svg (42 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states the task (class composition across conditions), notes all bars sum to 1, and gives two specific numerical findings (Class C ~44% in Cond2, ~41% in Cond5) plus a qualitative observation about Cond4.

overall_strengths: Clear, well-labelled stacked bar chart that directly addresses the brief; percent labels inside segments make values readable without needing to estimate from the axis.
Caption is publication-quality: it describes what is shown, how to read it, and includes specific numerical claims tied to named conditions.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0/none): fonts are Type3 bitmaps in the PDF and converted to paths in the SVG, making text non-selectable and non-editable in vector outputs.
Red-green palette conflict: Class B (green #55A868) and Class C (red #C44E52) are indistinguishable for red-green colorblind readers, violating colorblind-safe design requirements.
